title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: csdata CRAN-published R package providing structural reference data Norway: population age/municipality, location hierarchies (ward/municipality/county/region), location names, redistricting mappings 2006 2024. data versioned geographic border year (2020 2024), controlled via csdata::config$border_nor.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: unit tests (tests/ directory exist). package relies R CMD check CI validation.
code: # Full R CMD check (same as CI) R CMD build . && R CMD check csdata_*.tar.gz --no-manual --as-cran  # Install locally for testing Rscript -e 'devtools::install()'  # Generate documentation from roxygen2 comments Rscript -e 'devtools::document()'  # Run package check via devtools Rscript -e 'devtools::check()'  # Build pkgdown site Rscript -e 'pkgdown::build_site()'

title: CLAUDE.md
text: GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/check--pkgdown.yml) runs push main/develop PRs main: 1. R-CMD-check ---manual ---cran 2. pkgdown site build deploy GitHub Pages (push main ) pushing, run gh run watch monitor CI.

what: Data flow: raw files -> internal datasets -> exported functions
title: CLAUDE.md
text: Raw source files data-raw/files/ — Excel/CSV files population data SSB, location spreadsheets, BA-region mappings Data-raw scripts (data-raw/01_*.R data-raw/04_*.R) — process raw files internal datasets, run numeric order Internal datasets stored R/sysdata.rda — named border year (e.g., nor_locations_hierarchy_b2020, nor_population_by_age_b2024) Exported accessor functions R/ — function takes border parameter retrieves corresponding internal dataset via get0(..., envir = asNamespace("csdata"))

title: CLAUDE.md
text: data-returning functions accept border = csdata::config$border_nor (default 2024). Internal datasets duplicated per border year (_b2020, _b2024). set_config(border_nor=) function changes package-wide default.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Location codes follow pattern {granularity}{number}: nation_nor, county_nor03, municip0301, wardoslo030101, baregion001, missingmunicip9999. prefix determines granularity_geo value.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: regenerate R/sysdata.rda, run data-raw/ scripts order. Intermediate results cached data-raw/data-temp/ .rds files. Population data fetched SSB via PxWebApiData package.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Package uses data.table throughout (dplyr). internal data manipulation uses data.table idioms: :=, setnames, setcolorder, copy(). S3 methods used location_code_to_* add_*_to_data_set functions, dispatching data.table vs default. Norwegian special characters accessed via nb helper list (nb$aa, nb$oe, nb$ae uppercase variants). Roxygen2 markdown enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)).

title: csdata
text: Valid locations location types available csdata::nor_locations_names(). Entries uncommon internal use shown strikethrough.

title: csdata
text: Ages must coded characters must always contain three digits. age ranges, join two ages underscore (e.g. 005_010). Use 085p rather >=085 85+; keeps conversion long wide format straightforward. format keeps data sorted correctly produces valid variable names pivoted wide format. Missing ages coded "missing".

title: csdata
text: Sex must coded character. Missing sex coded "missing".

title: Population (Norway)
code: pd <- csdata::nor_population_by_age_cats()[granularity_geo %in% "nation" & calyear>=1990]  q <- ggplot(pd, aes(x=calyear, y = pop_jan1_n)) q <- q + geom_line() q <- q + geom_point(mapping = aes(color = imputed)) q <- q + scale_x_continuous("Calendar year (calyear)") q <- q + scale_y_continuous("Population as of Jan 1 (pop_jan1_n)") q <- q + scale_color_discrete("Imputed") q <- q + labs(title = "Norway (nation_nor)") q

title: Population (Norway)
text: tables show pop_jan1_n (population 1 January) location calendar year.

title: Authors and Citation
text: package proudly brought : Richard Aubrey White. Author, maintainer. Chi Zhang. Author. See changelog contributors. 🙏

title: Authors and Citation
text: White RA, Zhang C (2021). csdata: Structural Data Norway. R package version 2026.7.2, https://niphr.github.io/csdata/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {csdata: Structural Data for Norway},   author = {Richard Aubrey White and Chi Zhang},   year = {2021},   note = {R package version 2026.7.2},   url = {https://niphr.github.io/csdata/}, }

what: Add a granularity_geo column to a data set — add_granularity_geo_to_data_set
title: Add a granularity_geo column to a data set — add_granularity_geo_to_data_set
text: Derives geographic granularity label location_code column adds new granularity_geo column, modifying x place. location_reference NULL granularity inferred location code prefix; reference table supplied, looked directly.

title: Add a granularity_geo column to a data set — add_granularity_geo_to_data_set
Add a granularity_geo column to a data set — add_granularity_geo_to_data_set
text: x data.table containing column named location_code. location_reference data.table columns location_code granularity_geo use lookup. NULL (default), granularity derived location code prefix (e.g. "county_nor03" -> "county").

title: Add a granularity_geo column to a data set — add_granularity_geo_to_data_set
text: x, invisibly, granularity_geo column added updated.

title: Add a granularity_geo column to a data set — add_granularity_geo_to_data_set
code: library(data.table) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘data.table’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     %notin% data <- data.table(location_code = c("nation_nor", "county_nor03", "blah")) csdata::add_granularity_geo_to_data_set(data) print(data) #>    location_code granularity_geo #>           <char>          <char> #> 1:    nation_nor          nation #> 2:  county_nor03          county #> 3:          blah            blah  library(data.table) data <- data.table(location_code = c("nation_nor", "county_nor03", "blah")) csdata::add_granularity_geo_to_data_set(data, location_reference = csdata::nor_locations_names()) print(data) #>    location_code granularity_geo #>           <char>          <char> #> 1:    nation_nor          nation #> 2:  county_nor03          county #> 3:          blah            <NA>

what: Add an iso3 column to a data set — add_iso3_to_data_set
title: Add an iso3 column to a data set — add_iso3_to_data_set
text: Derives ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code location_code column adds new column, modifying x place. Currently Norwegian location codes map "".

title: Add an iso3 column to a data set — add_iso3_to_data_set
Add an iso3 column to a data set — add_iso3_to_data_set
text: x data.table containing column named location_code.

title: Add an iso3 column to a data set — add_iso3_to_data_set
text: x, invisibly, new granularity_geo column containing ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (always "" Norwegian locations).

title: Add an iso3 column to a data set — add_iso3_to_data_set
code: library(data.table) data <- data.table(location_code = c("nation_nor", "county_nor03", "blah")) csdata::add_iso3_to_data_set(data) print(data) #>    location_code granularity_geo #>           <char>          <char> #> 1:    nation_nor             nor #> 2:  county_nor03             nor #> 3:          blah             nor

title: Package configuration environment — config
text: environment stores package-level configuration variables. Modify via set_config(); read directly (e.g. config$border_nor).

title: Package configuration environment — config
text: Available variables: border_nor (default 2024): border year used selecting Norwegian geographic datasets. Valid values: 2024.

title: Package configuration environment — config
code: print(ls(csdata::config)) #> [1] "border_nor" for(i in names(csdata::config)){   cat(i, ":", csdata::config[[i]], "\n") } #> border_nor : 2024

what: Convert location codes to granularity_geo — location_code_to_granularity_geo
title: Convert location codes to granularity_geo — location_code_to_granularity_geo
text: Extracts geographic granularity label one location codes. location_reference NULL, granularity derived lowercase alphabetic prefix location code (e.g. "county_nor03" -> "county"); special prefix "norge" mapped "nation". reference table supplied, granularity looked directly.

title: Convert location codes to granularity_geo — location_code_to_granularity_geo
Convert location codes to granularity_geo — location_code_to_granularity_geo
text: x character vector location codes, data.table / data.frame containing column named location_code. location_reference data.table columns location_code granularity_geo use lookup. NULL (default), granularity inferred location code prefix.

title: Convert location codes to granularity_geo — location_code_to_granularity_geo
text: character vector length x (many elements rows x x data.table), containing corresponding granularity_geo values.

title: Convert location codes to granularity_geo — location_code_to_granularity_geo

what: Convert location codes to ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes — location_code_to_iso3
title: Convert location codes to ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes — location_code_to_iso3
text: Returns ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code location code. Currently Norwegian location codes map "".

title: Convert location codes to ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes — location_code_to_iso3
Convert location codes to ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes — location_code_to_iso3
text: x character vector location codes, data.table / data.frame containing column named location_code.

title: Convert location codes to ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes — location_code_to_iso3
text: character vector length x (many elements rows x x data.table), containing corresponding ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (always "").

title: Convert location codes to ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes — location_code_to_iso3
Norwegian characters in unicode — nb
text: named list Norwegian special characters unicode strings, use literal non-ASCII characters inconvenient. Elements: AA/aa (Aa/aa), OE/oe (Oe/oe), AE/ae (Ae/ae).

title: Location hierarchies in Norway — nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to
text: Returns mapping two geographic levels Norway. accept character vectors, case requested combinations returned combined single data.table.

title: Location hierarchies in Norway — nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to
code: nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to(   from,   to,   include_to_name = FALSE,   border = csdata::config$border_nor )

title: Location hierarchies in Norway — nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to
text: Character vector. source geographic granularity. One : "wardoslo", "extrawardoslo", "wardbergen", "wardtrondheim", "wardstavanger", "missingwardoslo", "missingwardbergen", "missingwardtrondheim", "missingwardstavanger", "municip", "baregion", "county", "georegion", "mtregion", "notmainlandmunicip", "notmainlandcounty", "missingmunicip", "missingcounty". Character vector. target geographic granularity. valid values . include_to_name Logical. TRUE, include name location third column to_name. Default FALSE. border Integer. geographic border year. Valid values: 2024. Defaults csdata::config$border_nor.

title: Location hierarchies in Norway — nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to
text: data.table columns: from_code Location code granularity level. to_code Location code granularity level. to_name Name location (present include_to_name = TRUE).

title: Location hierarchies in Norway — nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to
code: csdata::nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to(from = "wardoslo", to = "county") #>              from_code      to_code #>                 <char>       <char> #>  1: wardoslo_nor030101 county_nor03 #>  2: wardoslo_nor030102 county_nor03 #>  3: wardoslo_nor030103 county_nor03 #>  4: wardoslo_nor030104 county_nor03 #>  5: wardoslo_nor030105 county_nor03 #>  6: wardoslo_nor030106 county_nor03 #>  7: wardoslo_nor030107 county_nor03 #>  8: wardoslo_nor030108 county_nor03 #>  9: wardoslo_nor030109 county_nor03 #> 10: wardoslo_nor030110 county_nor03 #> 11: wardoslo_nor030111 county_nor03 #> 12: wardoslo_nor030112 county_nor03 #> 13: wardoslo_nor030113 county_nor03 #> 14: wardoslo_nor030114 county_nor03 #> 15: wardoslo_nor030115 county_nor03 csdata::nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to(from = "municip", to = "baregion") #> Empty data.table (0 rows and 2 cols): from_code,to_code csdata::nor_locations_hierarchy_from_to(   from = c("municip", "county"),   to   = "georegion",   include_to_name = TRUE ) #>            from_code        to_code                  to_name #>               <char>         <char>                   <char> #>   1: municip_nor0301 georegion_nor5     Østlandet-Austlandet #>   2: municip_nor1101 georegion_nor3               Vestlandet #>   3: municip_nor1103 georegion_nor3               Vestlandet #>   4: municip_nor1106 georegion_nor3               Vestlandet #>   5: municip_nor1108 georegion_nor3               Vestlandet #>  ---                                                         #> 368:    county_nor42 georegion_nor4                    Agder #> 369:    county_nor46 georegion_nor3               Vestlandet #> 370:    county_nor50 georegion_nor2    Trøndelag-Trööndelage #> 371:    county_nor55 georegion_nor1 Nord-Norge-Davvi-Norggas #> 372:    county_nor56 georegion_nor1 Nord-Norge-Davvi-Norggas

what: Location codes and names for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_names
title: Location codes and names for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_names
text: Returns data.table Norwegian geographic units location codes, display names, presentation metadata. Coverage includes nation, counties, municipalities, city districts (Oslo, Bergen, Stavanger, Trondheim), BA-regions, lab regions.

title: Location codes and names for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_names
Location codes and names for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_names
text: https://.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liste_over_norske_kommunenummer

title: Location codes and names for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_names
text: border Integer. geographic border year determining administrative boundaries used. Valid values: 2024. Defaults csdata::config$border_nor.

title: Location codes and names for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_names
text: data.table one row per geographic unit columns: location_code Location code (e.g. "nation_nor", "county_nor03"). location_name Full location name. location_name_short Abbreviated name: 1-letter nation county, shorter display name Oslo Bergen city districts. location_name_description_nb Location name parenthetical description geographic level (Norwegian Bokmal). location_name_file_nb_utf Name suitable use file names, retaining Norwegian characters. location_name_file_nb_ascii Name suitable use file names, Norwegian characters replaced ASCII equivalents. location_order Integer giving preferred presentation order. granularity_geo Geographic granularity: one "nation", "georegion", "mtregion", "county", "municip", "baregion", "wardoslo", "wardbergen", "wardstavanger", "wardtrondheim", "extrawardoslo", "lab".

what: Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
title: Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
text: Returns data.table weighting factors used convert historical data recorded old administrative boundaries 2024 borders. row maps original location code (given calendar year) current location code, proportional weighting.

title: Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
text: Statistics Norway (SSB) municipal reform documentation.

title: Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
text: border Integer. target geographic border year. Valid values: 2024. Defaults csdata::config$border_nor.

title: Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
text: data.table columns: location_code_current Location code target border year. location_code_original Location code existed calyear. calyear calendar year location_code_original applies. weighting Proportional weight apply aggregating original location current location (values sum 1 within location_code_original / calyear group). granularity_geo Geographic granularity: one "nation", "county", "municip", "wardbergen", "wardoslo", "wardstavanger", "wardtrondheim", "missingwardbergen", "missingwardoslo", "missingwardstavanger", "missingwardtrondheim", "notmainlandcounty", "notmainlandmunicip", "missingcounty".

title: Redistricting weights for Norwegian geographic units — nor_locations_redistricting
code: d <- csdata::nor_locations_redistricting() head(d) #>    location_code_current location_code_original calyear weighting #>                   <char>                 <char>   <num>     <num> #> 1:            nation_nor             nation_nor    1975         1 #> 2:            nation_nor             nation_nor    1976         1 #> 3:            nation_nor             nation_nor    1977         1 #> 4:            nation_nor             nation_nor    1978         1 #> 5:            nation_nor             nation_nor    1979         1 #> 6:            nation_nor             nation_nor    1980 & granularity_geo == "municip"] #>      location_code_current location_code_original calyear weighting #>                     <char>                 <char>   <num>     <num> #>   1:       municip_nor0301        municip_nor0301    2019         1 #>   2:       municip_nor1101        municip_nor1101    2019         1 #>   3:       municip_nor1103        municip_nor1103    2019         1 municip_nor2028    2019         1 #> 429:       municip_nor5634        municip_nor2002    2019         1 #> 430:       municip_nor5636        municip_nor2027    2019         1 #>      granularity_geo what: Norwegian population aggregated into custom age categories — nor_population_by_age_cats
title: Norwegian population aggregated into custom age categories — nor_population_by_age_cats
text: Aggregates bundled Norwegian population dataset (Statistics Norway) caller-defined age bands. underlying data covers every integer age 0 105 national, county, municipality, city-district level.

title: Norwegian population aggregated into custom age categories — nor_population_by_age_cats
code: nor_population_by_age_cats(   cats = NULL,   include_total = TRUE,   include_9999 = FALSE,   border = csdata::config$border_nor )

title: Norwegian population aggregated into custom age categories — nor_population_by_age_cats
text: cats named unnamed list integer vectors specifying age values include category. vector element defines one age band. list element named, name used age label; otherwise label auto-generated "LLL_HHH" (zero-padded lower upper bounds). Defaults NULL (custom bands; "total" returned include_total = TRUE). include_total Logical. TRUE (default), additional row group age = "total" covering ages appended. include_9999 Logical. TRUE, recent calendar year duplicated added calyear = 9999, following cstidy convention granularity_time == "event_*". Default FALSE. border Integer. geographic border year. Valid values: 2024. Defaults csdata::config$border_nor.

title: Norwegian population aggregated into custom age categories — nor_population_by_age_cats
text: data.table columns: granularity_geo Geographic granularity level. location_code Location code. age Age category label, derived cats names auto-generated, plus "total" include_total = TRUE. sex Sex. Always "total" current dataset. calyear Calendar year. pop_jan1_n Population count 1 January calyear. imputed Logical. TRUE value imputed.

title: Norwegian population aggregated into custom age categories — nor_population_by_age_cats
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Not run: aggregates the full population dataset, which exceeds CRAN's # example time limit.  # Default: return total population only d <- nor_population_by_age_cats() head(d[granularity_geo == "nation"])  # Named age bands d2 <- nor_population_by_age_cats(   cats = list("children" = 0:17, "working_age" = 18:66, "elderly" = 67:105),   include_total = TRUE ) d2[granularity_geo == "nation" & calyear == 2024] } # }

what: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
title: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
text: function categorizes Norwegian population custom age categories, split sex (male/female) optionally total sexes.

title: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
code: nor_population_by_sex_age_cats(   cats = NULL,   include_total_age = TRUE,   include_total_sex = TRUE,   include_9999 = FALSE,   border = csdata::config$border_nor )

title: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
text: cats list containing vectors want categorize. include_total_age Boolean. 'total' included age cat? include_total_sex Boolean. 'total' (sexes combined) included sex, alongside 'male' 'female'? include_9999 Boolean. current year duplicated added "calyear==9999". accordance cstidy principles regarding granularity_time=="event_*". border year Norwegian geographical boundaries designated (2024).

title: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
text: data.table containing following columns: granularity_geo location_code age (specified argument "cats") sex ("male", "female", "total" include_total_sex) calyear pop_jan1_n imputed

title: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
text: locations Statistics Norway provides sex breakdown (Svalbard, Jan Mayen, unknown), male female returned NA total holds real count. Note include_total_sex = TRUE output holds male, female total long format, summing pop_jan1_n across sex values double-counts. Filter single sex, sum male/female components.

title: Population in Norway by sex and age categories — nor_population_by_sex_age_cats
Swedish characters in unicode — se
text: named list Swedish special characters unicode strings, use literal non-ASCII characters inconvenient. Elements: OE/oe (Oe/oe), AE/ae (Ae/ae).

title: Set package configuration options — set_config
text: Updates one variables config environment. Call start script change default border year used data-returning functions.

title: Set package configuration options — set_config
text: border_nor Integer. Norwegian geographic border year use default. Valid values: 2024. Pass NULL leave unchanged.

title: Set package configuration options — set_config
text: NULL, invisibly. Called side effect updating config.

title: Set package configuration options — set_config
code: old <- csdata::config$border_nor csdata::set_config(border_nor = 2024) csdata::config$border_nor  # 2024 #> [1] 2024 csdata::set_config(border_nor = old)  # restore

title: Version 2026.7.2
text: Breaking: dropped support 2020 border year. _b2020 datasets removed every data-returning function now accepts border = 2024 (set_config(border_nor = 2020) border = 2020 now error). Use border = 2024. Population data now carries sex dimension. nor_population_by_age_b2024 includes real male/female/total splits (fetched SSB tables 07459 10826 Kjonn = TRUE). Locations Statistics Norway provides sex breakdown (Svalbard, Jan Mayen, unknown) carry total NA male/female. New exported function nor_population_by_sex_age_cats() returns population custom age categories split sex, include_total_age include_total_sex toggles. nor_population_by_age_cats() unchanged callers: now filters sex == "total" internally, output remains identical (total ). Note: redistricted sub-national levels sex rounded independently, male + female may differ total 3 people ~0.6% historical cells (exact national level).

title: Version 2026.3.30
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-31 Population data pipeline now fetches directly SSB PxWeb API (tables 07459 10826) instead manually downloaded CSV files. Including 2025 2026 population data. Municipality population data now uses actual historical data per municipality code instead crude split approximations merged municipalities. National population data sourced table 07459 (1986+), replacing defunct dataset 59322. Svalbard population data now fetched SSB table 07430 (2026), replacing static Excel file. Removed unused Nordic country population location Excel files.
